preview_text:
The model favors Colombia to win at 75%, a substantial margin over DR Congo's 4% chance. The underdog is given a low probability of victory under the statistical forecast, reflecting a large gap in the head-to-head outcome distribution.

The 20% draw probability suggests the match has some competitive structure despite the model's strong lean toward a Colombia result. A draw is plausible but less likely than a clear winner; the W/D/L spread is moderately wide rather than tightly bunched, indicating the model expects Colombia to control the fixture without necessarily predicting a runaway scoreline.

The model and market diverge notably on DR Congo's win probability. The sportsbook consensus sits at 12.6%, and Polymarket at 12.3%—roughly three times higher than the model's 4.3% estimate. Both sources align closely on Colombia and the draw. The gap suggests either the market prices in scenarios the model discounts or applies different weighting to tail outcomes. The dashboard does not claim to know which assessment is correct.
